name: superpowers
description: Discipline-GATE lane — a NARROW escalation, NOT the default for code work. oh-my-claudecode is the default work-style lane for all coding; route HERE only when the operator EXPLICITLY wants a structural discipline gate that OMC cannot replicate: (a) executing a multi-task plan where EACH task needs a fresh implementer + a MANDATORY spec+quality reviewer before the next task starts (subagent-driven-development — sp's one irreplaceable mechanism), (b) test-first authoring where a test MUST be seen to fail before any production code (TDD Iron Law), or (c) a verification / peer-review gate on a SPECIFIC diff or a 'done' claim. Do NOT route here merely because code is involved, a bug exists, or a plan is being made — general feature authoring, solo bug-fixing, root-cause tracing, and step-by-step planning all DEFAULT to oh-my-claudecode (its ralplan is a stronger consensus planner; its ralph/team-verify cover post-hoc verification). It governs HOW you work on code — NOT a document/slide/image domain (those are installed skills), and NOT raw parallel throughput or autonomous looping (that is oh-my-claudecode).

skills.name: discipline-lane
skills.description: Representative signals that route to superpowers (the session then picks the exact SP skill).
examples: write this feature test-first / make the failing test pass
about to fix a bug — find the root cause first, don't guess
verify this is actually done/correct before we ship
send a reviewer over this specific diff
turn this chosen direction into a step-by-step plan
이 버그 고치기 전에 근본 원인부터
어떤 방향이 나을지 비교 (no direction yet)
